blockingIssues.description: Level achievements are checked only in the monster-death flow via `this.achievementSystem.checkLevel(this.player.level)`. The feature spec requires real-time progress tracking from all sources, but level-based achievements will not unlock when the player levels from quest turn-ins or other non-kill XP sources until a later kill occurs.

The difficulty selector is only shown when `save.completedDifficulties.length > 0`. After beating demon_lord, `completedDifficulties` is updated in memory in `ZoneScene.onMonsterKilled()`, but there is no auto-save after that mutation. If the player presses ESC to return to menu, `returnToMenu()` saves first, but if the game is closed/crashes or the player expects the unlock toast to mean the new difficulty is immediately available on the next menu visit, the Continue flow can still skip the selector because the save file was last written before the boss kill. The feature requires Nightmare/Hell to become selectable after clear; gating selector visibility on persisted `completedDifficulties` makes unlock availability depend on an additional later save rather than the clear event itself.

Combat still computes dodge and crit from defender.stats.dex / attacker.stats.dex / attacker.stats.lck only, ignoring equipStats.dex and equipStats.lck. As a result, Emerald and Diamond socket bonuses do not flow through to dodge/crit despite the feature spec requiring gem stats to flow through getEquipmentStats() → CombatSystem.

Runtime loot generation only enforces guaranteed quality floors for monsters marked `isMiniBoss`. The dungeon final boss (`dungeon_abyss_lord`) is not marked `isMiniBoss`, and `generateLoot()` never reads `monster.lootTable`, so the promised 'final boss guarantees ≥1 rare+' and 'mid-bosses ≥1 magic+' behavior is not actually implemented in gameplay; it only exists in data/tests.

Autosaving inside the dungeon stores the player on `abyss_rift` at the dungeon portal coordinates (`60,58`), and `exitDungeon()` also returns there. The feature spec requires save/load inside the dungeon to return the player to the Abyss Rift entrance, but this implementation respawns them at the portal instead.

`migrateV1toV2()` does not initialize the required `companions` field for v1 saves. The feature spec explicitly requires missing fields to default to an empty companions collection, but the migration only fills inventory/equipment/stash/quests/exploration/achievements/homestead/settings/difficulty/dialogue/lore-related fields. Loading a true v1 save will still leave `save.companions` absent.

nonBlockingIssues.description: `toggleAchievement()` registers a new global `this.input.on('wheel', ...)` handler every time the panel opens, but never removes it when the panel closes or the scene shuts down. Reopening the panel stacks duplicate wheel listeners, causing repeated scroll steps and avoidable input-handler leaks.

`update()` increments shared progress once per matching achievement, so generic kill progress is advanced multiple times per call when several generic kill achievements are still locked. This makes progress values and UI bars drift from actual player actions (for example one kill can add multiple points toward `kill` progress), even though thresholds are eventually reached.

Physical and spell skill stat scaling still uses attacker.stats.str / attacker.stats.int rather than effective stats including equipStats.str / equipStats.int at the point of calculation. ZoneScene currently works around this by pre-baking some bonuses into Player.baseDamage/max mana via recalcDerived(), but the CombatSystem pipeline itself does not directly honor equipped primary stats, which is inconsistent with the commit/handoff claim that gem stats fully flow through getTypedEquipStats() → CombatSystem.

The `save() sets version to CURRENT_SAVE_VERSION (2)` test is only a placeholder: it constructs `new SaveSystem()` and asserts the constant equals 2, but never calls `save()` or verifies the object written to Dexie. This leaves the advertised save-stamping behavior untested despite the feature claiming coverage for it.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented save-position recovery, delayed persistence of runtime unlocks, achievement runtime patterns, and combat/stat pipeline caveats surfaced during endgame scrutiny.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Explicitly require browser/manual verification evidence in the handoff for UI-heavy features, not just tests/build output.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The achievement-panel review found the worker transcript only recorded test/build/typecheck commands despite the UI skill requiring visual browser verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when UI-facing features that also change combat/stat pipelines should be assigned to systems-worker or split into coordinated UI + systems features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: gem-socketing-ui was labeled ui-worker in features.json, but the implementation and transcript relied on systems-worker-style changes to CombatSystem, Player, and InventorySystem.
